title: orchis latifolia subsp. elata
intro: Orchis latifolia subsp. elata, also known as the tall bee orchid, is a subspecies of the orchid plant, belonging to the Orchidaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Orchis latifolia subsp. elata include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and grinding the roots, which can then be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
